heading: Duties of trial counsel and defense counsel
label: 949c Duties of trial counsel and defense counsel
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 949c, added , , , related to duties of trial counsel and defense counsel, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2610Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006
amendments: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 949c, added , , , related to duties of trial counsel and defense counsel, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2610Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006

children.heading: .—Trial Counsel
children.label: a .—Trial Counsel
children.text: The trial counsel of a military commission under this chapter shall prosecute in the name of the United States.
The accused shall be represented in the accused’s defense before a military commission under this chapter as provided in this subsection.
The accused may be represented by military counsel detailed under or by military counsel of the accused’s own selection, if reasonably available.section 948k of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is admitted to the practice of law in a State, district, or possession of the United States, or before a Federal court;

children.label: C
children.text: has not been the subject of any sanction of disciplinary action by any court, bar, or other competent governmental authority for relevant misconduct;

children.label: D
children.text: has been determined to be eligible for access to information classified at the level Secret or higher; and

children.label: E
children.text: has signed a written agreement to comply with all applicable regulations or instructions for counsel, including any rules of court for conduct during the proceedings.
If the accused is represented by civilian counsel, military counsel shall act as associate counsel.

The accused is not entitled to be represented by more than one military counsel. However, the person authorized under regulations prescribed under to detail counsel, in such person’s sole discretion, may detail additional military counsel to represent the accused.section 948k of this title
Defense counsel may cross-examine each witness for the prosecution who testifies before a military commission under this chapter.

Civilian defense counsel shall protect any classified information received during the course of representation of the accused in accordance with all applicable law governing the protection of classified information, and may not divulge such information to any person not authorized to receive it.
